topic: Deep-attack c3 on SEC-6.13 Part 6.13 post-EXP-427 (kernel-of-truth h3) + c2 h1-h5 patch integration
trigger_note: Queue item 387 (class: deep-attack) — EXP-427 kernel-of-truth h3 inserted at top + all five c2 minor holes (h1-h5) self-applied by decider. Fresh deep-attack on fully integrated Part 6.13 prose (17,608 bytes, 5 h3 subsections, 17 paragraphs).
our_argument_summary: Part 6.13 (post EXP-427 + c2-patch integration) now opens with a kernel-of-truth h3 acknowledging the dome author had an internal 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label that would have produced a coherent (if weaker) home for the imported MOND-grouping items. The body sustains the four-h3 architecture: (1) the list of eleven WINs from the late-March 2026 commit burst and why list-match-without-derivation is borrowing not corroboration; (2) the closed-world / square-cube argument that the dome's bounded firmament (~10^7 m extent) is dimensionally incompatible with galactic-scale physics; (3) the third-option-retreat closure (galaxies-as-illusions still cannot produce confirmations); (4) category placement / self-own (the WIN-048 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label was used inconsistently); (5) a deeper-lesson coda on real-science legitimacy. Five WINs (047 Hubble, 048 CMB Axis, 052 RAR, 054 El Gordo, 055 Cepheid) remain reclassified to Self-Contradicted; all five verdicts verified against data/wins.json at review time. The c2 patches (h1-h5) have all landed correctly — verbatim diff confirms the third-option-retreat closure now ends with the 'no firmament-projection derivation' clause, the NFW framework-collapse phrasing is in place, the 'galactic-and-extragalactic' framing is applied, the local-vs-Planck Hubble-tension comparison is added to the observable list, and the WIN-052 cross-reference is now an HTML anchor link to #win052.
proposed_section_summary: No changes proposed in this review — this is a verify-and-deep-attack pass on integrated content. Section state at review time: 17,608 bytes (within Part 6 size band 3.3-22.8 KB), 5 h3 subsections, 17 paragraphs, TLDR 929 chars.

relevant_files: data/wins.json: WIN-029 (firmament-height self-contradiction), WIN-044/045/046/047/048/049/050/051/052/053/054/055 (the eleven MOND-burst WINs and adjacent tidal/internal-derivation items, all currently Self-Contradicted)
monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-6.13-mond-grouping.c2.json (prior cycle review whose h1-h5 patches are now verified integrated)
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json: ISS-2115 (h5 from c1 closed by EXP-427), ISS-2128/2129/2130/2131/2132 (h1-h5 from c2 closed by decider-self-apply-same-run)
raw-text/02-wins.txt, raw-text/04-coordinates.txt, raw-text/05-model.txt (verifying the WIN-029 three-firmament-heights claim)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Prose verification; no numerical pipeline. Integration checks performed: (1) confirmed kernel-of-truth h3 inserted at top of <div class="ps-detail"> before existing 'The list, and why list-match matters' h3 — matches EXP-427.replacement_html structure; (2) confirmed all five c2 patches landed verbatim by grep on the integrated section text; (3) confirmed all five reclassified WINs (047/048/052/054/055) still verdict='Self-Contradicted' in data/wins.json; (4) confirmed WIN-029 detail_evidence cites three firmament-height values (8,537 from formula, 9,086 from parameter, 9,572 from quarter-wave Schumann derivation); (5) cross-checked the section's claim of three values 'across model.html, coordinates.html, and the predictions registry' against raw-text — model.html lists all three internally, coordinates.html lists 8,537/9,086/'~9,500 km' (approximation, not 9,572), predictions registry lists only 9,572; the one-value-per-document framing is loose.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — section-prose review, not WIN-level code validation.
data_sources_used: McGaugh, Lelli & Schombert 2016 PRL 117.201101 (RAR original) — cited in section
Mistele et al. 2024 ApJL 969 L3 (DOI 10.3847/2041-8213/ad54b0) — cited in section
Asencio, Banik & Kroupa 2023 ApJ 954 162 (DOI 10.3847/1538-4357/ace62a) — cited in section
Banik & Zhao 2022 Symmetry 14(7):1331 (DOI 10.3390/sym14071331) — cited in section
data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence (the 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label — load-bearing for the new kernel h3)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The closed-world h3's third-option-retreat closure (post c2-h1 patch) now ends with 'and the dome has produced no derivation from firmament-projection mechanics that would substitute for it on any of these specific signatures (see the preceding paragraphs and WIN-052 detail).' The 'see the preceding paragraphs' cross-reference points to the square-cube argument, which establishes the impossibility of 3D mass distributions in the dome — but does NOT establish the absence of firmament-projection-mechanics derivation attempts. The promised cross-reference is genuinely unmet by the cited paragraphs. See Hole h2 (moderate) below.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: WIN-029 attribution: the section's claim that 'three inconsistent peak values...across model.html, coordinates.html, and the predictions registry' implies a clean one-per-document distribution. Reality (per raw-text/04-coordinates.txt, raw-text/05-model.txt, raw-text/02-wins.txt): model.html lists all three values internally; coordinates.html lists 8,537/9,086/~9,500 km (the '9,500' is an approximation, not 9,572); the predictions registry (wins.txt summary line) lists only the 9,572 derivation. Also: WIN-029's own detail_evidence flags only TWO internally-inconsistent values (8,537 and 9,086, 6.4% discrepancy 'never acknowledged'). The 9,572 is a derived Schumann-quarter-wave value, not a separately-claimed dome constant. See Hole h3 (moderate) below.

code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section is our review's argument, not a dome claim.

holes_found.description: Kernel/self-own h3 redundancy creates a pacing problem. The new kernel-of-truth h3 (inserted by EXP-427) says 'the author had the right concept available...The category-error here is not that the items are wrong to surface; it is that they are surfaced under the strongest-claim category (confirmed WINs, tallied into the headline 69 Confirmed Predictions scorecard on the home page) when the author own taxonomy contained a more accurate one. That placement is where the self-own enters.' But the later 'The category placement, and the self-own' h3 makes the same observation at length: the WIN-048 ΛCDM Counter-Prediction sub-label is the same load-bearing evidence, the same 'available and was sometimes used (WIN-048 carries the label). It was not used for the rest of the grouping' argument, and the same 'author chose the strongest available claim category for material that does not warrant it' conclusion. A reader reaches the third h3 having already heard the punchline; rhetorical pacing is off. The c1-h5 fix that triggered EXP-427 wanted the kernel led with — but the EXP did not tighten the later self-own h3 to match, leaving two h3s arguing the same point with different exposition lengths. Defender response: 'your section says the same thing twice — once briefly and once at length — which suggests neither is doing real work.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either tighten the kernel h3 to acknowledge the sub-label EXISTS and was used for WIN-048 (deferring the 'placement is the self-own' conclusion to the dedicated h3), or trim the later self-own h3's first two paragraphs to avoid restating the kernel. Preferred option: tighten kernel h3 to ~120 words by dropping the 'category-error here is not that...' sentence and the headline-scorecard mention, both of which the self-own h3 develops in more depth. This preserves the EXP-427 fulfillment of c1-h5 (kernel is led with) without producing the awkward call-and-response between kernel and self-own.

holes_found.description: The c2-h1 patch ('see the preceding paragraphs') points to paragraphs that don't actually establish the cited claim. The added clause now reads: 'and the dome has produced no derivation from firmament-projection mechanics that would substitute for it on any of these specific signatures (see the preceding paragraphs and WIN-052 detail).' But the preceding paragraphs in the same h3 establish that 3D mass distributions cannot exist in the bounded firmament (square-cube scaling collapses RAR to formal undefined-ness) — they do NOT establish the absence of any firmament-projection-mechanics derivation. A defender clicking back will find an argument that the 3D-physics route is impossible, but no audit of whether the dome author has EVER published any firmament-projection mechanism for these specific signatures. The c2 closure of h1 was structurally right (closing the third-option retreat by invoking the unestablished mechanism) but the citation target is wrong — it promises the preceding paragraphs cover the no-derivation point and they don't.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'see the preceding paragraphs' with a more accurate citation. Option A: 'see the no-derivation-chain argument earlier in this subsection,' pointing back to the 'There is no derivation chain from the disc radius to RAR's a₀ threshold' paragraph in 'The list, and why list-match matters' h3 — which IS the right reference. Option B: append one half-sentence: 'and no firmament-projection mechanism for these specific signatures has been published in the dome registry (data/wins.json: WIN-044 and WIN-053 are the only dome-internal derivations attempted, and neither addresses RAR, El Gordo lensing, CMB alignment, Hubble tension, or Cepheid tension).' Option A is shorter; Option B is more rigorous.

holes_found.description: The WIN-029 self-contradiction citation overreaches. The section claims 'the dome\'s own published documents listing three inconsistent peak values — 8,537, 9,086, and 9,572 km — across model.html, coordinates.html, and the predictions registry (data/wins.json:WIN-029 documents this self-contradiction; dome coordinates.html).' Verification against raw-text: (1) raw-text/05-model.txt internally lists 8,537 (formula at r=0), 9,086 (model parameterization average), and 9,572 (Schumann quarter-wave derivation) — all three appear in this one document; (2) raw-text/04-coordinates.txt lists 8,537, 9,086, and 'Schumann (~9,500 km)' — the 9,500 is an explicit approximation, not 9,572; (3) raw-text/02-wins.txt lists only the 9,572 derivation in the WIN-029 summary. Two issues: (a) the one-value-per-document framing implied by 'across [three documents]' doesn't match the actual distribution (most values appear in multiple documents); (b) the 9,572 is a DERIVED value from the quarter-wave Schumann formula, not a separately-claimed dome constant in the sense that 8,537 (formula at r=0) and 9,086 (model parameter) are — and WIN-029 detail_evidence itself flags only the 8,537-vs-9,086 contradiction as 'never acknowledged,' treating the 9,572 as the Schumann claim the dome made to assert the conductive ceiling. A dome defender can argue: '9,572 isn\'t a third inconsistent value; it\'s the value Schumann would require IF the dome bounds the cavity, which is the WIN-029 claim itself — you\'re double-counting our claim as a contradiction.'
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to match what WIN-029 actually documents: 'the dome\'s own published documents listing two inconsistent peak values — 8,537 km (formula at r=0 from H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619)) and 9,086 km (model parameterization average) — never reconciled (data/wins.json:WIN-029 detail_evidence). A third derivation, 9,572 km from the quarter-wave Schumann formula H = c/(4f), is what the dome cites as the Schumann ceiling but doesn\'t match either parameter value.' This is accurate to WIN-029, removes the defender's double-counting line, and preserves the 'inconsistency' argument that's load-bearing here.

holes_found.description: The kernel h3 grants ground that the closed-world h3 then needs to recapture. The kernel says applying the ΛCDM Counter-Prediction sub-label consistently to the four cosmological items 'would have produced a defensible — if much weaker — claim structure.' But the later closed-world h3 argues none of the five can survive ANY framing because the bounded firmament cannot accommodate galactic-scale physics. The kernel's 'defensible — if much weaker' concession is in tension with the closed-world argument that NO framing rescues them. A smart defender will quote the kernel back: 'even your reviewer admits my framing was defensible; you\'re just bickering about which weaker version I should have chosen.' The section can\'t both concede 'defensible if differently-framed' AND argue 'incoherent regardless of framing' — it has to pick.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften the kernel concession: 'would have produced a less indefensible — though still ultimately incoherent under the dome\'s bounded-firmament commitments documented below — claim structure.' Or, more surgically: 'would have produced a more honest claim structure (still wrong on the closed-world argument, but at least not asserting confirmation).' This preserves the EXP-427 kernel framing while removing the 'defensible' word that the closed-world h3 will spend the next 2,000 words demolishing.

holes_found.description: 'Stacy McGaugh maintains it in varying forms on his Triton Station blog' is a weak citation. Triton Station is McGaugh's actual blog (tritonstation.com — link present in the section), but there is no single canonical anomaly-list post; it's a running commentary across many posts. A defender or curious reader could request the specific URL and find none. The same paragraph already has the Banik & Zhao 2022 Symmetry citation (DOI 10.3390/sym14071331) which IS a published canonical survey of MOND-supporting anomalies. The Triton Station handwave dilutes the much stronger Banik & Zhao citation.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop the Triton Station sentence entirely, OR replace with a specific URL/post if one exists, OR pin to a published McGaugh review (e.g., McGaugh 2020 'Predictions and outcomes for the dynamics of rotating galaxies' Galaxies 8(2):35, DOI 10.3390/galaxies8020035, which is the closest thing to a canonical McGaugh anomaly-survey paper). The Banik & Zhao citation alone is sufficient to support the 'circulates in the MOND-curious sphere as the canonical set' claim.

holes_found.description: '69 Confirmed Predictions' headline count needs verification against the current dome home page. The kernel h3 says 'tallied into the headline 69 Confirmed Predictions scorecard on the home page.' raw-text/01-home.txt is dated 2026-03-12 (V50.6) and shows '39 Confirmed Wins' — already stale by ~2 months. The dome model is now V51.1 per CLAUDE.md (69 confirmed). If the dome has updated to V51.2 or higher since this prose was drafted, the quoted number may no longer match, giving a defender a 'they\'re not reading my current site' gotcha. Quoting a specific number ties the prose to a specific dome version; the argument doesn't need the specific number to land.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) verify the current dome home-page count from a fresh fetch and pin to that exact number with a date stamp ('as of YYYY-MM-DD'), or (b) generalize to 'tallied into the headline confirmed-predictions scorecard on the home page' without a specific count. Option (b) is robust against future dome updates and costs nothing argumentatively. The kernel h3's punch is 'placement under the strongest-claim category,' not 'placement among the 69 items.'

holes_found.description: 'Plasma cosmology' in the deeper-lesson h3 risks guilt-by-association. The paragraph reads: 'MOND commits to galactic-scale 3D physics. Plasma cosmology commits to electromagnetic structure across cosmic distances. Modified-inertia models commit to their own dimensional architectures.' MOND is mainstream-fringe (peer-reviewed, debated seriously in the literature); plasma cosmology (Alfvén-Klein-Peratt tradition) is much more marginal and is associated with fringe-adjacent communities the dome author may actually overlap with rhetorically. A dome defender can argue: 'you\'re smearing MOND-grouping by lumping it with plasma cosmology — these have very different scientific standing.' The argument works fine with MOND and modified-inertia alone, both of which are dimensional-architecture-bearing without plasma cosmology's marginal-program baggage.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop the plasma-cosmology line. Replace with 'MOND commits to galactic-scale 3D physics. Modified-inertia models commit to their own dimensional architectures. Even less-favored alternatives like emergent-gravity formulations (Verlinde 2017) commit to specific scales at which their formulas apply.' Or simpler: just keep MOND and modified-inertia, drop the third example. The list-of-three rhythm is rhetorical, not load-bearing.

stronger_arguments.description: The deeper-lesson h3 could land harder by naming the asymmetry. Real cosmological alternatives (MOND, modified-inertia, TeVeS, scalar-tensor, even plasma cosmology) all require the universe to be bigger than the dome allows — but they also all require specific dimensional commitments that the dome model doesn't even have a vocabulary for. A clean way to land this: 'The fringe of real cosmology has dimensional commitments. The dome model does not have dimensional commitments at cosmological scales because it denies cosmological scales exist. Borrowing a cosmological-scale anomaly list while denying cosmological scales is the structural error.' This is sharper than the current 'cosmology cannot be smaller than its sources of legitimacy' phrasing — which is good but doesn't name the dimensional-vocabulary gap.
sources: Internal SEC-6.13 closed-world h3 (the 10^7 m firmament extent argument)

stronger_arguments.description: The section could note that the dome's own 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label is itself a tell: a model that needs a sub-category for 'ways the alternative is in trouble, separate from ways my model is right' is implicitly admitting that the alternative-in-trouble items are doing different argumentative work. The dome author saw this clearly enough to coin the sub-label — and then chose not to apply it consistently. The kernel-of-truth h3 hints at this but doesn\'t name the sub-label's existence as itself diagnostic of the author's awareness. A one-sentence add: 'That the dome author coined the sub-label at all is evidence he understood the structural distinction — which makes the inconsistent application not an oversight but a choice.' This sharpens the self-own argument without lengthening the section much.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence (the explicit ΛCDM Counter-Prediction label)
Internal SEC-6.13 'The category placement, and the self-own' h3

stronger_arguments.description: The third-option-retreat closure could be tightened by noting that it generalizes: any future cosmological-scale anomaly the dome imports will face the same dilemma, regardless of which observable it concerns. The current closure addresses RAR, El Gordo, Cepheid, CMB-alignment, and Hubble-tension specifically. Adding one sentence — 'This dilemma applies to any photon measurement of a putatively extragalactic object, including BAO standard rulers, supernova Hubble diagrams, and gravitational-wave standard sirens that the dome registry may import in future commits' — forecloses the most likely future dome-author move (adding more MOND-community anomalies). Prophylactic against future borrowing.
sources: Internal SEC-6.13 closed-world h3 (the bounded-firmament dimensional argument)
data/wins.json:WIN-047/055 (Hubble tension and Cepheid ladder, both within local + distance-ladder cosmology)

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-427 kernel-of-truth h3 correctly identifies that the dome author had the right rhetorical concept available — his own published WIN-048 prose uses the 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label, which IS a coherent way to file 'ways the alternative is in trouble' separately from 'ways my model is right' (data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence). The kernel preserves what's true about the dome author's own taxonomy: he saw the structural distinction clearly enough to coin a sub-label for it (the WIN-048 prose is verbatim 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction'), and at least one of the eleven MOND-burst WINs (WIN-048 itself) carries the label correctly. The kernel is also rhetorically right that Part 6 comparator subsection p6-614-kappa-cluster establishes the 'lead with what the dome cannot abandon' pattern that the original SEC-6.13 c1 prose violated by burying the kernel in the fourth h3. The dome's own taxonomic vocabulary genuinely contained a better home for the imported anomalies than the WIN registry, and acknowledging that before dismantling the placement choice IS the credible-reviewer move.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Conceding the dome author had the right vocabulary doesn't change the closed-world dimensional argument that follows: the dome's ~10^7 m firmament extent is geometrically incompatible with the galactic-scale physics the imported anomalies are about, regardless of which sub-label slot the dome author files them under (data/sections.json:part6 p6-613-mond-grouping 'Why the closed-world commitment forecloses the derivation route' h3). The kernel illuminates why the placement-as-WIN is a self-own (the author had the better option and chose worse) rather than merely a mistake — but the underlying five WINs (047/048/052/054/055, all verdict='Self-Contradicted' in data/wins.json) fail under both framings: the WIN framing fails because the dome doesn't predict the observables in the first place; the 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' framing fails because the dome doesn't have a competing prediction-generating mechanism that could be tested against the same observables. The kernel reframes WHY the placement matters but doesn't rescue any of the five reclassified WINs from the Self-Contradicted verdict the closed-world argument forces.

text_fingerprint.verdict_assertions_about_wins: WIN-047/048/052/054/055 reclassified Self-Contradicted (verified in data/wins.json at review time)
text_fingerprint.integration_status: EXP-427 kernel-of-truth h3 inserted + all five c2 patches (h1 moderate, h2-h5 minor) verbatim-confirmed integrated

deeper_analysis: The c3 review confirms the section is in good structural shape post-integration. EXP-427 fulfilled the c1-h5 'kernel buried' moderate hole by inserting a dedicated kernel-of-truth h3 at the top, matching the Part 6.13 comparator p6-614-kappa-cluster's lead-with-the-load-bearing-concept pattern. The five c2 patches landed cleanly: the third-option closure now explicitly invokes the no-firmament-projection-derivation point, the NFW framework-collapse phrasing avoids the three-parallel-frameworks mislabeling, the galactic-and-extragalactic framing covers the Cepheid ladder, the local-vs-Planck Hubble-tension comparison restores one-to-one observable coverage with WIN-047/048/052/054/055, and the WIN-052 cross-reference is an HTML anchor. The reclassified WINs all still carry verdict='Self-Contradicted' (verified against data/wins.json at review time). New holes flagged in this cycle are calibration issues, not foundational: h1 (kernel/self-own redundancy, moderate) and h2 (h1 patch citation target wrong, moderate) and h3 (WIN-029 three-values claim overreaches, moderate) are the three moderates; h4 (kernel concession too generous), h5 (Triton Station weak citation), h6 (69 confirmed predictions count needs date-stamp), h7 (plasma cosmology guilt-by-association) are the four minors. None require verdict change. None require a major rewrite. All seven are addressable by targeted prose edits. The cumulative pattern — three c2 minors became c3 moderates because their REPLACEMENT text introduced new problems — is worth the decider's attention: patch-the-patch dynamics indicate the underlying prose has accumulated load-bearing weight that's brittle to small edits. Recommend the decider triage h1/h2/h3 as patch candidates (or escalate one of them to a small EXP if the prose work exceeds a sed-replace) and leave h4-h7 as wontfix-or-defer per the standard minor-disposition rule. The cross-WIN consistency check (all five MOND-grouping WINs verdict='Self-Contradicted') is the strongest positive finding — the section's central claim about which WINs the analysis covers is verified at the data level. Advocate-mode counterargument considered: a sophisticated dome defender's strongest move at this stage is to argue that 'compatibility' (the dome's actual rhetorical claim in the MOND-grouping detail_evidence fields) is a weaker but meaningful claim that doesn't require derivation — and the section's response is the third-option-retreat closure, which IS the right answer but could be sharper (per stronger_arguments[2] above). Confidence dropped to 0.78 (from c2's 0.84) reflecting the three moderates plus the meta-observation that patches-of-patches is a brittleness signal.
